pages.text: Novus has twenty villager professions — thirteen of them vanilla, seven added by mods, all of them claimed the same way: an unemployed villager picks the nearest unowned workstation and the trade begins.$(br2)What follows groups them by the room of the village they belong in, not by the mod they came from. Workstation block in italics; trade specialty after.
pages.title: Professions

pages.text: $(bold)The Homestead$() — the field, the barn, the kitchen, the garden.$(br)$(li)$(bold)Farmer$() ($(italic)Composter$()) — bread, pies, golden carrots; buys raw crops$(li)$(bold)Fisherman$() ($(italic)Barrel$()) — cooked fish, enchanted rods, campfires$(li)$(bold)Butcher$() ($(italic)Smoker$()) — cooked meat, sweet berries, dried kelp$(li)$(bold)Shepherd$() ($(italic)Loom$()) — wool, dyes, paintings, beds$(li)$(bold)Cook$() ($(italic)Cooking Pot$()) — broths and simple stews$(li)$(bold)Chef$() ($(italic)Stove$() / $(italic)Skillet$()) — pies, glazed dishes, harder FD recipes$(li)$(bold)Horticulturist$() ($(italic)Flower Tub$()) — seeds, saplings, flowers, dyes
pages.title: The Homestead

pages.text: $(bold)A note on the Shepherd.$() Higher-tier Shepherds in Novus will also sell you $(italic)live farm animals$() — chickens, pigs, cows, sheep, the occasional rabbit. The remedy if you spawn in a biome with no fauna; lethal to your emeralds if you let the trade list run.$(br2)Other professions occasionally sell animals too, but the Shepherd is the reliable one.
pages.title: Sold by the Shepherd

pages.text: $(bold)The Workshop$() — metal, stone, leather, wood.$(br)$(li)$(bold)Armorer$() ($(italic)Blast Furnace$()) — iron and chain armor, enchanted iron pieces$(li)$(bold)Toolsmith$() ($(italic)Smithing Table$()) — iron tools, enchanted diamond tools, bells$(li)$(bold)Weaponsmith$() ($(italic)Grindstone$()) — iron axes and swords, enchanted diamond ones$(li)$(bold)Mason$() ($(italic)Stonecutter$()) — stone variants, terracotta, glazed terracotta; also $(italic)Ash Bricks$()$(li)$(bold)Carpenter$() ($(italic)Saw$()) — axes (every tier), boats, hanging signs, armor stands, logs, pistons$(li)$(bold)Fletcher$() ($(italic)Fletching Table$()) — bows, crossbows, tipped arrows$(li)$(bold)Leatherworker$() ($(italic)Cauldron$()) — leather armor, leather horse armor, saddles
pages.title: The Workshop

pages.text: $(bold)The Hall$() — paper, potions, prayers, charts.$(br)$(li)$(bold)Librarian$() ($(italic)Lectern$()) — enchanted books, name tags, paper, lanterns, clocks$(li)$(bold)Cleric$() ($(italic)Brewing Stand$()) — potions, ender pearls, redstone, glowstone, lapis, bottle o' enchanting$(li)$(bold)Cartographer$() ($(italic)Cartography Table$()) — maps and banner patterns; also $(italic)Adventurer Maps$(), $(italic)Antique Ink$(), $(italic)Pathfinder's Quills$()$(li)$(bold)Alchemist$() ($(italic)Alchemist Table$()) — potions, brewing reagents (blaze powder, ghast tears)$(li)$(bold)Occultist$() ($(italic)Enchanted Basin$()) — enchanted books at higher tiers, XP-bearing trinkets$(li)$(bold)Oceanographer$() ($(italic)Aquarium$()) — nautilus shells, prismarine, fish, coral, treasure maps
pages.title: The Hall

pages.text: $(bold)The Nitwit.$() One villager skin you'll see often and never trade with: the $(italic)Nitwit$(), green-robed, profession-less. He claims no workstation, holds no trades, and rolls fresh into the role whenever the village runs short on jobs — there's no curing him.$(br2)Treat him as set-dressing. Or, if you have the room and the patience, breed past him to bring the population back up.
pages.title: The Nitwit

pages.text: $(bold)Looking up the trades.$() Hold the recipe viewer's key and search a profession name. $(italic)Just Enough Professions$() answers with the workstation block and the complete trade list at every tier — vanilla professions and the seven modded ones alike.$(br2)There is no other clean way to read the new professions' trade tables in-game.
pages.title: Looking Up Trades
